channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Your ATT&CK Heatmap Is Counting Rules, Not Coverage
item.description: Every detection vendor ships a MITRE ATT&CK heatmap, and every one of them is mostly green. Broad coverage, techniques lit up across the board, a reassuring wall of color in the sales deck and the...

item.title: WSL 2: Die wichtigsten Befehle für deinen Entwickler-Alltag (Cheatsheet)
item.description: Wer täglich in anspruchsvollen Agenturprojekten arbeitet und zwischen Windows-Host und Linux-Umgebung wechselt, weiß: WSL 2 ist ein Gamechanger. Doch selbst wenn man das perfekte Setup einmal...

item.title: React + Mapbox GL JS: Custom Markers, Popups, and Bounds-Based Data Fetching
item.description: React + Mapbox GL JS: Custom Markers, Popups, and Bounds-Based Data Fetching If you've added a Mapbox map in a React app before, you've probably hit a wall pretty quickly: Mapbox GL JS manages its...

item.title: What is an LLM evaluation harness? A deep dive into lm-eval-harness
item.description: What is an LLM evaluation harness? A deep dive into lm-eval-harness You fine-tuned a 7B model. It aced your smoke tests, your colleague ran a few prompts and shrugged approvingly, and the README...

item.title: Building a Self-Healing Data Pipeline with Event-Driven Idempotence
item.description: Building a Self-Healing Data Pipeline with Event-Driven Idempotence Building a Self-Healing Data Pipeline with Event-Driven Idempotence A senior engineer’s sketchbook: a project I shipped to...

item.title: Vercel cron alternative: what to use when built-in cron isn't enough
item.description: Vercel's built-in cron triggers your serverless functions on a schedule. For simple use cases it works. But it has no failure alerts, no execution history on the Hobby plan, and no way to know...

item.title: Comment your stack — I will tell you what I would check first on a webhook bug
item.description:
Swap debugging war stories

I have been living in webhook + PHP + email land (Vapi, OpenAI, PHPMailer, CSV logs).

Drop your stack in a comment (even one line). I will reply with the first...

item.title: Stop shipping a 1990s C library to compute planets. Xalen is the pure-Rust, Apache-2.0 replacement for Swiss Ephemeris.
item.description: Stop shipping a 1990s C library to compute planets. Xalen is the pure-Rust, Apache-2.0 replacement for Swiss Ephemeris. If your app does astrology, you already know the dependency. Swiss...

item.title: Who else builds alone and nobody offline understands the grind?
item.description:
Not a job application — a peer search.

I ship PHP/JS/AI production sites. People around me cannot relate to webhook failures at 2am.

I want friends who are better coders than me in some...

item.title: I Finally Finished My AI Interview Coach (It Only Took Me Getting Rejected to Care)
item.description: This is a submission for the GitHub Finish-Up-A-Thon Challenge What I Built An AI interview coach that runs in your browser. No backend. No accounts. No subscriptions. You bring a free API key,...

item.title: The Download: Trump’s new AI order, and smart glasses for warfare
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. 5 key points in Trump’s new AI order Less than two weeks...

item.title: SwitchBot’s acquisition of Nanoleaf is about more than lighting
item.description: Smart lighting company Nanoleaf has been acquired by OneRobotics, the parent company of SwitchBot. In an exclusive interview with The Verge, Nanoleaf CEO Gimmy Chu says the company will remain...

item.title: Shrinking the IAM Attack Surface through Identity Visibility and Intelligence Platforms (IVIP)
item.description: The Fragmented State of Modern Enterprise Identity Enterprise IAM is approaching a breaking point. As organizations scale, identity becomes increasingly fragmented across thousands of...

item.title: Beyond the Zero-Day: See Your Network Like an Attacker | Webinar with HD Moore
item.description: Assume the breach. Zero-days keep shipping, AI is writing exploits faster than anyone patches, and "patch everything in time" stopped working years ago. Stop betting the org on winning that race....

item.title: AI Used to Decrypt Medieval Ciphers
item.description: Researchers are using machine learning algorithms to decrypt historical pencil-and-paper ciphers.

item.title: Nvidia’s RTX Spark Laptops Look Hell-Bent on Disruption
item.description: The company’s RTX Spark chips might finally turn the “AI PC” into reality.

item.title: Trans teens have something to say
item.description: By the time the Children's Hospital closed its doors to trans patients, Sage had already stopped taking testosterone. A nonbinary high school student, they originally received treatment for the...

item.title: Unpatched Windows Search URI Vulnerability Lets Attackers Steal NTLMv2 Hashes
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of an unpatched issue that could be exploited to disclose a user's NTLMv2 hash to the attacker. Like in the case of CVE-2026-33829, which impacted...

item.title: What’s Worth More Than Cash in San Francisco Real Estate? Anthropic Stock
item.description: Several real estate listings in the San Francisco Bay Area are offering to exchange a home for a piece of the AI startup.

item.title: Redditors Are Using AI to Beat Obscene World Cup Ticket Prices
item.description: Soccer fans on r/WorldCup2026Tickets are using Claude to build DIY ticketing software, exchanging on back channels, and leaving scalpers scrambling.

item.title: AI has a water problem. Google thinks it has a fix
item.description: In the face of widespread backlash to the AI data center buildout throughout the US, Google is touting its efforts to minimize the environmental impact by actually increasing water for local...

item.title: Google must let publishers opt out of AI Search features, rules UK
item.description: Online publishers are getting more control over whether their websites appear in Google's AI Search features, thanks to a UK regulatory ruling. The new conduct rule imposed by the Competition and...

item.title: New HTTP/2 Bomb Vulnerability Allows Remote DoS on NGINX, Apache, IIS, Envoy & Cloudflare
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a remote denial-of-service exploit that affects major web servers, including NGINX, Apache HTTPD, Microsoft IIS, Envoy, and Cloudflare Pingora. The...

item.title: Weedhack Attacks Minecraft Users, CountLoader Hits 86K, Miners Spread via Pirated Content
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new campaign targeting Minecraft players via YouTube to spread malware capable of gaining control of victims' systems. The Minecraft-focused...

item.title: Cyera eyes $12B valuation at 80x ARR multiple despite operating losses
item.description: The cybersecurity company is nearing a $300 million round led by Evolution Equity Partners.

item.title: God of War Laufey is coming to the PS5
item.description: Sony ended its big State of Play showcase with a major reveal: the next God of War. The new title is called God of War Laufey, and is once again developed by Sony's Santa Monica Studio. Currently,...

item.title: Remedy’s Control sequel launches in September
item.description: Control Resonant, the upcoming sequel to Remedy Entertainment's Control, will be released on September 24th, 2026, according to a trailer that premiered during Tuesday's PlayStation State of Play...

item.title: Here’s seven bloody minutes of Wolverine on the PS5
item.description: At its big State of Play show on Tuesday, Sony shared new look Marvel's Wolverine, the next big title from Insomniac Games that's launching exclusively on PS5 on September 15th. Dressed in the...

item.title: PlayStation State of Play June 2026: All the news and trailers
item.description: While the majority of Summer Game Fest takes place over the weekend, Sony is getting a jump on things with its next State of Play showcase, which airs on June 2nd at 5PM ET. It’s coming at a...

item.title: I held the next-gen handheld
item.description: Intel couldn't catch a break. Layoffs. Shakedowns. Crashing CPUs torpedoing its reputation, sending desktop gamers fleeing to AMD. Apple and Qualcomm pushing Intel out of multiple flagship...
